mission.title: Install and Configure SurrealDB
mission.description: SurrealDB is a scalable, distributed, collaborative, document-graph database designed for the real-time web. It is suitable for applications requiring flexible data modeling and high performance. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and management compared to traditional databases.

steps.title: Add the SurrealDB Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official SurrealDB Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add surrealdb https://charts.surrealdb.com

helm repo update

```

> This is the official repository for SurrealDB Helm charts.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install SurrealDB with Helm
steps.description:
This command installs SurrealDB in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install surrealdb surrealdb/surrealdb --namespace surreal --create-namespace --version 3.0.2

```

> Installs SurrealDB in the 'surreal' namespace.

steps.title: Create an example configuration
steps.description:
This step creates a sample configuration for SurrealDB to use.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: surrealdb.com/v1
kind: SurrealDB
metadata:
  name: surrealdb-example
  namespace: surreal
spec:
  database: 'test'
  username: 'admin'
  password: 'password'
EOF

```

> Creates a sample SurrealDB instance with basic credentials.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: surrealdb.com/v1
kind: SurrealDB
metadata:
  name: surrealdb-example
  namespace: surreal
spec:
  database: 'test'
  username: 'admin'
  password: 'password'
EOF

steps.title: Verify the SurrealDB installation
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the SurrealDB pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n surreal

```

> You should see the SurrealDB pod running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall SurrealDB
uninstall.description:
This step removes SurrealDB and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall surrealdb --namespace surreal

kubectl delete namespace surreal

```

> Uninstalls the SurrealDB release.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade SurrealDB
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This step will upgrade SurrealDB to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl exec -it <surrealdb-pod-name> -n surreal -- surrealdb backup --output /backup

helm upgrade surrealdb surrealdb/surrealdb --namespace surreal --version 3.0.2

kubectl get pods -n surreal

```

> Backup your current SurrealDB data.
> Upgrades SurrealDB to the specified version.
> Verify that the upgraded pod is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to database'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The SurrealDB pod is not running or is in a crash loop.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <surrealdb-pod-name> -n surreal' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Permission denied'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The user credentials provided in the configuration are incorrect.

**Fix:**
Verify the username and password in your SurrealDB configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Database not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified database does not exist in the SurrealDB instance.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the database name in your configuration matches an existing database.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes cluster does not have enough resources allocated for SurrealDB.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource limits for the SurrealDB deployment or scale your cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced new indexing features and improved query performance.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 2.x, ensure to migrate your data using the provided backup and restore commands.

resolution.summary: You should see the SurrealDB pod running with the status 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n surreal'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
